summary: Matched stage function grInishie2_801FD4F0 by reducing a PAD_STACK size from 16 to 12 in src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c, moving a local Vec3 stack slot from 0x24(r1) to the expected 0x20(r1). PR body reports objdiff target=100.0/current=100.0, and the bot report records one new match for main/melee/gr/grinishie2.

key_files.role: Touched stage translation unit containing grInishie2_801FD4F0; only changed PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12).

what_changed.effect_reported: PR body says this corrected the local Vec3 stack position from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1), taking the function from 99.91% fuzzy to 100.0%.

smart_moves.lesson: A tiny PAD_STACK adjustment can close a near-match when the remaining difference is a local stack-slot offset.
smart_moves.why_it_worked: The author identified the stack pad as 4 bytes too large and tied the change to the Vec3 moving to the expected stack offset.

smart_moves.lesson: The PR kept the change narrowly scoped to one line in the target function.
smart_moves.why_it_worked: Changed-file metadata and diff show one added line and one deleted line in src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function is already highly fuzzy-matched and the remaining mismatch is a local variable stack offset, inspect PAD_STACK sizing before changing logic or types.
Record the concrete stack-offset consequence of stack-padding changes, not just the final match percentage.

naming_conventions.convention: No naming change was made. Existing names such as gp, jobj, temp_r29, temp_r30, GET_JOBJ, and GET_GROUND were only visible as diff context.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted PAD_STACK from 16 to 12 to change local stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: Moved local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1), matching grInishie2_801FD4F0 at 100.0%.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used objdiff as the verifier for the claimed match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: PR body reports objdiff target=100.0/current=100.0; bot report shows grInishie2_801FD4F0 before 99.91%, after 100.00%.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Reported GALE01 matched-code progress of +0.01%, +348 bytes, with one new match: main/melee/gr/grinishie2 grInishie2_801FD4F0 from 99.91% to 100.00%.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: The PR is small and the loaded diff directly shows the only source change.
The PR body explicitly states the matching rationale and objdiff result.
The bot report independently records the same symbol moving from 99.91% to 100.00%.
No human review comments were available, so no reviewer intent or requested changes are inferred.
The intake does not independently rerun objdiff; it records the PR body and bot report as historical evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK size can control local stack-slot offsets in near-matched functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2643, grInishie2_801FD4F0 was reportedly 99.91% fuzzy because PAD_STACK was 4 bytes too large. Changing PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12) moved a local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1), and the PR reports objdiff target=100.0/current=100.0.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that PAD_STACK should be reduced whenever a function is fuzzy; the evidence supports only this specific stack-layout fix.
Do not infer naming improvements from temp_r29/temp_r30 context; no names were changed.
Do not claim independent validation beyond the PR body's objdiff statement and the bot report.
